calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Efforts To Provide Enhancements To The Beneficiary'S Life
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Benefits To Beneficiary
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Crisostomo Ibarra for and with Lawson Tom who appeared via court call.

The Court stated that this is a status on providing enhancement, that there is a plan in place and asked if the conservatee has been on an outing yet. Mr. Ibarra stated that they were waiting for approval from the court. The Court stated that it was not necessary to wait for approval, that the court will not pre-approve expenses and that it is just looking for this to happen. Mr. Tom stated that he believes his brother will enjoy the outings and that they will get started. The Court stated that this is a step in the right direction, that it would encourage other things as well and that it is up to the trustee to come up with ideas. The status on benefits to the beneficiary and status on efforts to provide enhancements to the beneficiary's life are off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Crisostomo Ibarra for Lawson Tom.

The Court addressed the issue that funds are available and that something should be done to enhance the beneficiaries quality of life with them. Mr. Ibarra stated that he will discuss this with Mr. Tom and that Mr. Tom lives in Canada and the sisters do not have the energy to take care of him. The Court stated that there are programs that could be utilized and set a status date of August 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an update. The Court also questioned the charging of professional fees for administrative services and extensive legal research on notice requirements. Mr. Ibarra stated that there is not much guidance from the administrator and that the paralegal does everything. The Court stated that it does not allow the charging of professional rates for secretarial activities, charging for services not incurred (messenger services) or presenting invoices for not true and correct work. The Court granted the petition re accounting (subject to review of the order) and subject to the adjustment of attorney's fees.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Second Account Of The Special Needs Trust And Report And Petition For Its Settlement, For Allowance Of Attorney Fees And For Payment Of Fees To Trustee.
Off Calendar Apr-22-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Crisostomo Ibarra for Lawson Tom (Petitioner).

Mr. Ibarra presented oral argument to support his position with regard to his request for additional fees and the amount of time billed for preparation of the one year accounting. The Second Account of The Special Needs Trust, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Accounting #1
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-05-287444 Conservatorship of Allan Tom.

Appearances: Crisostomo Ibarra for Lawson Tom.

Mr. Crisostomo reported that the Conservatee was receiving Social Security benefits, that the Conservatee was moved to Victoria Manor, and that he believed that Ms. Fong was pursuing the issue of loss of benefits and the $39,000.00 claim. Mr. Crisostomo also addressed the issue of Mr. Piccinini's fees as well as his own. The Court expressed concern about Lawson Tom paying Attorney's fees without Court authorization/a Court order and his ability to continue in his roll as Conservator. Mr. Lawson is not to pay any Attorney's fees without a Court order and Mr. Crisostomo is not to accept any payment of fees without a Court order. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy F. Andrade.
